page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highcpu-72 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It features 72 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 144.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, aligning with compute-intensive design patterns where processing power is prioritized over large memory footprints.

The instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached persistent disks. Additionally, it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The absence of integrated storage and graphics hardware makes this instance highly specialized for pure CPU-bound workloads, avoiding the added cost of unused hardware components.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4a-highcpu-72 offers high compute density for workloads that scale horizontally across many cores without requiring massive memory allocations. It is suited for workloads such as high-performance web serving, batch processing, distributed analytics, and compute-heavy compilation pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-highcpu-72 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance designed for high-density processing workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 72 dedicated vCPUs and 144.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is optimized for pure CPU-bound tasks, offering qualitative cost efficiency by eliminating the overhead of unneeded local storage or graphics hardware. The c4a-highcpu-72 is suited for compute-intensive applications, including batch processing, distributed analytics, web serving, and compilation pipelines.
